$comment: D-CSUBSET-EXTERN-DEFINITION-MERGE: an `extern` declaration + a same-TU definition of the same name MERGE (the definition wins; the extern's import row is suppressed) — for OBJECTS (`extern int g; int g = 30;`) and FUNCTIONS (`extern int add(int,int);` + body), in both orders. add(g,12)+h = 30+12+0 = 42. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the Pass-1 extern merge -> `extern int g;` + `int g = 30;` collide S_RedeclaredSymbol -> the program no longer COMPILES (compile failure, not exit 42). The release arm re-witnesses under the shipped pipeline (ordinary globals + a function; no extern import row survives for the locally-defined symbols).
